title: Applicability of the Act
description: The DPDPA 2023 applies to the processing of digital personal data within India, and to processing of personal data outside India if done in connection with any activity related to offering goods/services to data principals within India.
purpose: Defines the scope and territorial applicability of India's data protection framework.
implementationGuidance: Determine whether the Act applies to your organisation. If you process personal data of Indian data principals (inside or outside India), the Act applies. Map all data flows involving Indian residents' personal data.
requiredDocuments: Data flow mapping for Indian personal data
evidenceExamples: Data flow maps showing India-related personal data
bestPractices: Conduct applicability assessment with legal counsel
Map all processing activities involving Indian residents' data
Review applicability when entering Indian markets or changing business models

title: Grounds for Processing Personal Data
description: Personal data of a data principal may be processed only for a lawful purpose for which the data principal has given consent, or for certain legitimate uses specified in the Act.
purpose: Establishes the legal grounds for processing personal data under DPDPA.
implementationGuidance: Identify and document the lawful basis for every personal data processing activity. For consent-based processing, implement compliant consent mechanisms. For legitimate uses, document the applicable provision. Maintain a Record of Processing Activities.
requiredDocuments: Processing purposes register with lawful basis
evidenceExamples: Processing register showing lawful basis for each activity
bestPractices: Document the lawful basis during processing activity design
Apply consent as lawful basis only when appropriate
Regularly review processing activities against their stated lawful basis

title: Notice to Data Principal
description: Before or at the time of seeking consent, the data fiduciary must provide the data principal with a notice specifying the personal data to be collected and the purpose of processing.
purpose: Ensures data principals are informed about data collection and processing.
implementationGuidance: Provide clear, understandable notices to data principals before or at the point of data collection. Notices must state: what personal data is being collected, the purpose of processing, and how to exercise rights. Notices must be available in English and any scheduled language of the Constitution of India.
bestPractices: Use plain language in all notices
Provide notices in the data principal's preferred language where possible
Review notices whenever processing activities change

title: Valid Consent Requirements
description: Consent under DPDPA must be free, specific, informed, unconditional, and unambiguous with a clear affirmative action. Consent must be limited to specified personal data and purpose. Consent can be withdrawn at any time.
purpose: Ensures consent under DPDPA is meaningful and reflects genuine choice.
implementationGuidance: Implement consent mechanisms that are free, specific, informed, unconditional, and unambiguous. Do not bundle consent with terms of service. Allow withdrawal of consent as easily as giving it. Maintain consent records.
requiredDocuments: Consent records with timestamp and scope
evidenceExamples: Consent capture UI showing affirmative action requirement
bestPractices: Do not pre-tick boxes or use deceptive interfaces
Store granular consent records with timestamp and version of notice
Process consent withdrawal promptly

title: Consent Manager
description: A data principal may give, manage, review, and withdraw consent through a Consent Manager — a registered entity that acts as a single point of contact to enable consent management.
purpose: Provides data principals with a centralised mechanism for managing their consent.
implementationGuidance: If using consent through a Consent Manager, ensure the Consent Manager is registered with the Data Protection Board. Maintain consent records accessible through the Consent Manager. Honour withdrawal processed through Consent Managers.
requiredDocuments: Consent record accessibility via Consent Manager
Withdrawal processing via Consent Manager procedures
evidenceExamples: Consent records accessible via Consent Manager
Withdrawal processing records from Consent Manager
bestPractices: Monitor the Consent Manager registration requirements under DPDPA Rules
Integrate with registered Consent Managers to simplify data principal consent management
Ensure consent records are transferable if switching Consent Managers

title: Legitimate Uses Without Consent
description: The Act specifies certain legitimate uses for which personal data may be processed without consent, including: performance of State functions, compliance with law/court orders, medical emergency, employment purposes, and public interest.
purpose: Defines the legitimate use exceptions to the consent requirement.
implementationGuidance: Identify processing activities that qualify as legitimate uses. Document the specific provision under Section 7 that applies. Do not over-rely on legitimate uses as a substitute for consent when consent would be more appropriate.
requiredDocuments: Section 7 provision justification for each legitimate use
evidenceExamples: Legal analysis supporting each legitimate use claim
Review records for legitimate use processing
bestPractices: Document the specific Section 7 provision for each legitimate use activity
Have legal counsel review legitimate use determinations
Periodically review if consent can be obtained instead of relying on legitimate use

title: General Obligations of Data Fiduciary — Accuracy
description: A data fiduciary shall make reasonable efforts to ensure that the personal data of a data principal is accurate, complete, and consistent with the purposes for which it is processed.
purpose: Ensures data quality is maintained to prevent harm from inaccurate data.
implementationGuidance: Implement data quality controls for personal data. Provide mechanisms for data principals to update their data. Conduct periodic data accuracy reviews.
bestPractices: Implement automated data quality checks at the point of data input
Allow data principals to easily update their personal data
Conduct periodic data quality audits

title: Data Minimisation and Purpose Limitation
description: A data fiduciary shall collect only such personal data as is necessary for the specified purpose, retain it only for the period necessary, and shall not retain personal data beyond that period.
purpose: Implements data minimisation and purpose limitation principles.
implementationGuidance: Collect only personal data necessary for the stated purpose. Define retention periods and implement deletion at end of retention. Regularly review data collected to identify and remove excess data.
bestPractices: Apply data minimisation as a design principle
Automate deletion of personal data at end of retention period
Annually review all personal data collected to identify what is no longer necessary

title: Reasonable Security Safeguards
description: A data fiduciary shall implement reasonable security safeguards to prevent a personal data breach.
purpose: Requires implementation of appropriate technical and organisational security measures.
implementationGuidance: Implement security safeguards appropriate to the sensitivity of the personal data processed. Include technical controls (encryption, access controls, monitoring) and organisational controls (policies, training, vendor management). Document all security measures.
bestPractices: Align security safeguards with internationally recognised standards (ISO 27001, NIST CSF)
Conduct annual security risk assessments
Implement layered security controls proportionate to the sensitivity of data processed

title: Personal Data Breach Notification
description: In the event of a personal data breach, the data fiduciary shall inform the Board and each affected data principal of the breach in such manner as may be prescribed.
purpose: Ensures timely notification of breaches to regulators and affected individuals.
implementationGuidance: Implement breach detection and notification procedures. Notify the Data Protection Board and affected data principals upon discovery of a breach. Maintain breach records. Note: breach notification timelines will be specified in Rules.
bestPractices: Implement breach detection capabilities to identify breaches promptly
Pre-draft breach notification templates
Conduct breach response exercises to test notification procedures

title: Processing of Personal Data of Children
description: A data fiduciary shall not process personal data of a child without verifiable parental consent. A data fiduciary shall not process personal data that is likely to be detrimental to the well-being of a child. No behavioural monitoring or targeted advertising to children.
purpose: Provides enhanced protection for children's personal data under DPDPA.
implementationGuidance: Implement age verification and parental consent mechanisms for services accessible to children. Prohibit behavioural monitoring, targeted advertising, and tracking of children. Assess services for child accessibility and apply enhanced protections accordingly.
evidenceExamples: Prohibition of child behavioural monitoring controls
bestPractices: Apply privacy-by-design principles specifically for child-accessible services
Use verifiable parental consent mechanisms, not self-declaration
Prohibit all targeted advertising to users identified as children

title: Significant Data Fiduciary Obligations
description: Data fiduciaries designated as Significant Data Fiduciaries (SDFs) by the Central Government based on volume/sensitivity of data processed must comply with additional obligations including appointing a Data Protection Officer, conducting DPIAs, and data audits.
purpose: Imposes enhanced obligations on organisations with large-scale or sensitive data processing.
implementationGuidance: Monitor the Central Government's designation of Significant Data Fiduciaries. If designated as an SDF, appoint a DPO who reports to the Board of Directors. Implement DPIAs for high-risk processing. Engage an independent data auditor. Register with the Board.
evidenceExamples: DPO appointment and reporting line documentation
bestPractices: Monitor Central Government notifications for SDF designations proactively
Appoint a qualified DPO with data protection expertise if designated as SDF
Conduct DPIAs for all high-risk processing regardless of SDF status

title: Rights of Data Principal — Access
description: A data principal is entitled to: (a) a summary of personal data processed and the processing activities; (b) identities of all data fiduciaries with whom data has been shared; (c) grievance redressal.
purpose: Provides data principals with the right to know how their data is being used.
implementationGuidance: Implement processes to receive and fulfil data principal information requests. Provide a summary of personal data processed and processing activities. Disclose third-party sharing upon request. Establish a grievance redressal mechanism.
evidenceExamples: Processing summary provided to data principals
bestPractices: Implement a self-service portal for data principal requests
Respond to access requests within a defined timeframe (Rules will specify)
Maintain clear records of all third-party sharing for access request responses

title: Right to Correction, Completion, and Erasure
description: A data principal has the right to correct, complete, update, or erase personal data where no longer necessary for the purpose for which it was processed.
purpose: Enables data principals to exercise control over their personal data.
implementationGuidance: Implement processes to receive and fulfil correction, completion, update, and erasure requests. Verify requests before implementation. Propagate changes to data processors. Maintain request records.
requiredDocuments: Correction, update, and erasure request procedures
bestPractices: Implement data correction and erasure capabilities in all major data systems
Respond to correction and erasure requests within a defined timeframe
Log all data changes with before/after state for accountability

title: Right of Grievance Redressal
description: A data principal shall have the right to have grievances redressed by the data fiduciary. Every grievance shall be addressed expeditiously and in a manner prescribed.
purpose: Provides data principals with a mechanism to raise and resolve privacy concerns.
implementationGuidance: Establish a formal grievance redressal mechanism with a designated contact. Define response timelines (Rules will specify). Track and resolve all grievances. Maintain grievance records.
bestPractices: Publish grievance contact details prominently in privacy notices
Set and meet response timelines for grievances
Use grievances to improve privacy practices

title: Right to Nominate
description: A data principal shall have the right to nominate any other individual who shall, in the event of death or incapacity of the data principal, exercise the rights of the data principal.
purpose: Enables exercise of privacy rights on behalf of deceased or incapacitated data principals.
implementationGuidance: Implement nomination mechanisms for data principals. Define procedures for nominee verification upon invocation. Define what rights nominees can exercise on behalf of the data principal.
bestPractices: Implement digital nomination mechanisms in data management portals
Verify nominee identity rigorously before exercising rights
Define clear procedures for handling nominee requests

title: Duties of Data Principal
description: A data principal shall not impersonate another person while providing personal data, suppress any material information while providing personal data, and shall not register false grievances.
purpose: Establishes reciprocal obligations on data principals.
implementationGuidance: Include data principal duties in terms of service and privacy notice. Implement technical controls to verify identity and prevent impersonation. Define procedures for handling false or malicious data principal requests.
requiredDocuments: Data principal duties in terms of service
evidenceExamples: Terms of service with data principal duties
bestPractices: Include data principal duties in privacy notice and T&C
Implement robust identity verification for data subject requests
Define consequences for data principals who misuse their rights

title: Data Protection Board of India
description: The Data Protection Board of India is established to adjudicate non-compliance with DPDPA, determine the occurrence of personal data breaches, and impose penalties.
purpose: Establishes the regulatory authority for DPDPA enforcement.
implementationGuidance: Monitor Board establishment and published guidance. Register as a Significant Data Fiduciary if designated. Cooperate with Board investigations. Implement complaint handling processes aligned with Board procedures.
bestPractices: Monitor Data Protection Board guidance and decisions proactively
Maintain a compliance evidence library for Board investigations
Engage legal counsel for Board interaction

title: Penalties
description: The DPDPA provides for significant financial penalties for non-compliance. Maximum penalties include up to ₹250 crore for failure to implement security safeguards, up to ₹200 crore for failure to notify breaches, and up to ₹10,000 for data principal duty violations.
purpose: Provides the enforcement framework to incentivise DPDPA compliance.
implementationGuidance: Understand the penalty framework and prioritise controls addressing the highest-penalty obligations. Implement robust security safeguards (up to ₹250 crore risk) and breach notification (up to ₹200 crore risk). Use penalty exposure to justify compliance investment.
bestPractices: Calculate maximum penalty exposure to prioritise compliance investment
Implement security safeguards (highest penalty risk) and breach notification as top priorities
Document compliance efforts for use as mitigating factors in enforcement

title: Transfer of Personal Data Outside India
description: The Central Government may restrict transfer of personal data by a data fiduciary to certain countries as notified.
purpose: Provides the Central Government with authority to restrict international data transfers.
implementationGuidance: Monitor Central Government notifications on restricted countries. Map all cross-border data transfers. Implement controls to block transfers to restricted countries. Comply with sector-specific data localisation requirements.
evidenceExamples: Transfer register showing all international data flows
Technical controls for restricted country transfers
bestPractices: Monitor Central Government notifications on restricted jurisdictions proactively
Map all international data transfers including cloud processing locations
Implement data residency controls for sensitive categories of personal data

title: Data Protection Officer Appointment (SDF)
description: Significant Data Fiduciaries must appoint a Data Protection Officer who is a person resident in India and represents the data fiduciary before the Board. The DPO must report to the Board of Directors.
purpose: Ensures SDFs have a qualified, accountable privacy leader.
implementationGuidance: If designated as an SDF, appoint a qualified DPO who is a resident of India. Ensure the DPO reports directly to the Board of Directors or equivalent highest managerial body. Publish DPO contact details.
bestPractices: Appoint a DPO with DPDPA expertise
Ensure DPO has direct board access
Publish DPO contact in all privacy notices and on company website

title: Data Protection Impact Assessment (SDF)
description: Significant Data Fiduciaries must conduct periodic Data Protection Impact Assessments in respect of any of its activities involving the processing of personal data.
purpose: Identifies and mitigates high-risk processing activities for significant data fiduciaries.
implementationGuidance: Develop a DPIA methodology and trigger criteria. Conduct DPIAs for all high-risk processing activities. Document DPIA outcomes and implement recommended controls. Review DPIAs periodically.
bestPractices: Conduct DPIAs during system design, not retrospectively
Use DPIA findings to drive security and privacy control improvements
Track all DPIA recommendations to closure

title: Periodic Data Audit (SDF)
description: Significant Data Fiduciaries must have an independent data audit performed to evaluate the compliance of the data fiduciary with the provisions of DPDPA.
purpose: Provides independent assurance of SDF DPDPA compliance.
implementationGuidance: Engage an independent, qualified data auditor. Define the audit scope covering all DPDPA obligations. Implement audit findings promptly. Report audit results to the DPO and Board.
bestPractices: Engage an auditor with expertise in Indian data protection law
Include all DPDPA obligations in the audit scope
Remediate all audit findings within defined timeframes

title: Data Processor Obligations
description: A data processor processes personal data only on behalf of a data fiduciary under a contract and for the purpose specified by the data fiduciary.
purpose: Defines the obligations of entities processing data on behalf of others.
implementationGuidance: If acting as a data processor, process personal data only as instructed by the data fiduciary. Do not use personal data for own purposes. Implement appropriate security measures. Notify data fiduciary of breaches.
requiredDocuments: Breach notification procedures to data fiduciary
evidenceExamples: Processor activity records showing compliance with instructions
Breach notification records to data fiduciaries
bestPractices: Document all processing activities performed on behalf of data fiduciaries
Implement security measures equivalent to those required by data fiduciaries
Notify data fiduciaries of breaches promptly to enable their Board notification obligations

title: Grievance Redressal Mechanism
description: Data fiduciaries must establish a published grievance redressal mechanism that responds to data principal complaints expeditiously.
purpose: Provides a formal, accessible channel for data principals to raise privacy concerns.
implementationGuidance: Establish a grievance officer and a formal complaints procedure. Publish contact details. Define response timelines. Track all complaints and their resolution. Report on complaint trends to management.
bestPractices: Publish grievance contact details on the company website and in all privacy notices
Set a maximum 30-day response target for all grievances
Use complaint trends to identify and address systemic privacy issues

title: Consent Withdrawal and Its Effect
description: A data principal may withdraw consent at any time. Upon withdrawal, the data fiduciary shall cease processing within a reasonable time. Withdrawal does not affect the legality of processing before withdrawal.
purpose: Enables data principals to withdraw consent and have processing cease.
implementationGuidance: Implement consent withdrawal mechanisms that are as easy to use as consent. Process withdrawals within a reasonable time (Rules will specify). Cease all consent-based processing upon withdrawal. Notify data processors of consent withdrawal.
bestPractices: Process consent withdrawals within 72 hours as a target
Implement one-click withdrawal for digital services
Notify all processors of consent withdrawal within 24 hours

title: Exemptions from DPDPA
description: DPDPA provides exemptions for certain activities including national security, prevention/investigation/prosecution of offences, research/archiving/statistical purposes, and processing by data principals for personal and domestic purposes.
purpose: Defines the boundaries of DPDPA applicability.
implementationGuidance: Assess whether any of your processing activities qualify for exemptions. Document the exemption basis for each qualifying activity. Do not over-rely on exemptions without proper legal analysis.
bestPractices: Seek legal counsel before claiming exemptions
Document exemptions with specific legal basis
Review exemption applicability periodically

title: Privacy Notice Contents
description: DPDPA notices must clearly state the personal data sought to be collected, the purpose of processing, and how data principals can exercise their rights.
purpose: Ensures privacy notices provide the minimum required information.
implementationGuidance: Review all privacy notices against DPDPA notice requirements. Ensure notices cover: data collected, processing purpose, rights available, and how to exercise them. Provide notices in clear, plain language in English and relevant Indian languages.
requiredDocuments: Privacy notice review against DPDPA requirements
evidenceExamples: Privacy notice reviewed against DPDPA Section 5 requirements
bestPractices: Have legal counsel review notices against DPDPA requirements
Provide notices in the data principal's preferred Indian language
Update notices whenever processing activities change

title: Personal Data Breach Response
description: Data fiduciaries must have a process to detect, assess, and respond to personal data breaches, and must notify the Data Protection Board and affected data principals.
purpose: Ensures breaches are detected and reported promptly.
implementationGuidance: Implement breach detection capabilities (SIEM, DLP, monitoring). Define breach assessment procedures. Prepare notification templates for the Board and data principals. Conduct breach response exercises annually.
bestPractices: Implement 24/7 security monitoring to detect breaches quickly
Pre-draft breach notification templates for both Board and data principals
Conduct annual breach response exercises

title: Data Processor Management
description: Data fiduciaries must ensure data processors handle personal data only as instructed and are subject to appropriate contractual obligations.
purpose: Ensures the data processor ecosystem meets DPDPA requirements.
implementationGuidance: Identify all data processors. Execute processing agreements with all processors. Include mandatory obligations: process only as instructed, implement security safeguards, assist with data principal rights, delete data on instruction, notify of breaches. Conduct periodic processor reviews.
bestPractices: Execute processing agreements with all processors before sharing personal data
Conduct annual reviews of critical processors' compliance
Include audit rights in all processing agreements

title: Privacy Training and Awareness
description: All personnel handling personal data should be trained on DPDPA requirements and their obligations.
purpose: Ensures staff can recognise and fulfil their DPDPA obligations.
implementationGuidance: Develop DPDPA training covering key obligations, data principal rights, breach notification, and the role of the Data Protection Board. Deliver at onboarding and annually. Provide role-specific training for high-risk roles.
bestPractices: Tailor training to role-specific DPDPA risks
Require annual refreshers covering regulatory updates
Test comprehension with post-training assessments

title: Record Keeping and Documentation
description: Data fiduciaries must maintain records of processing activities and compliance measures to be able to demonstrate DPDPA compliance.
purpose: Supports accountability by ensuring compliance can be evidenced.
implementationGuidance: Maintain comprehensive records of all processing activities, consent records, breach notifications, data principal request responses, and security measures. Implement version control. Retain records for a period aligned with Rules.
evidenceExamples: Breach and request records organised by date and subject
bestPractices: Use a purpose-built privacy management tool to maintain records at scale
Conduct quarterly records completeness audits
Structure records for rapid retrieval in response to Board investigations
